expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the project plan: cites specific, accurate details from clearcontract_project_plan.md (phases, schema, architecture decisions, or the Out of Scope list)
expectations.evidence: Cites the snapshot mechanism, quote_events with IP/user-agent, server-locked accepted_total, Stripe deposit path, Phase 4/5 placement, and proposes the accept payload change {method, name, agreed_terms} consistent with section 4.

expectations.text: Notices that quote e-signatures are explicitly listed in the plan's Out of Scope list (section 9)
expectations.evidence: "the plan already made a call on this: quote e-signatures are explicitly in the Out of Scope list (section 9). Your mate's comment is a good reason to re-examine that call, not automatically overturn it."

expectations.text: Presents at least two genuine alternatives to the user's proposal (a defended 'defer / do nothing' counts as one)
expectations.evidence: Three options: do nothing; typed-name + T&C checkbox (~0.5-1 day); full drawn/DocuSign e-signature (2-3 days or per-envelope fees).

expectations.text: Commits to a single clear recommendation rather than a neutral pros/cons list
expectations.evidence: "My recommendation: option 2, and don't delay anything for it... Skip option 3 entirely unless a real customer someday disputes an accepted quote."

expectations.text: Weighs build cost concretely for a solo part-time developer (time estimates or specific complexity costs like new collections/hooks)
expectations.evidence: "roughly half a day to a day inside Phase 4/5. One extra field in the accept payload, no new collections, no new statuses"; option 3 costed at 2-3 days plus mobile-conversion risk.

notes: Checked git state and reframed 'before I launch' as a Phase 4/5 design decision being made early.
Steelmanned the mate's concern (cash/bank-transfer accept paths are evidentially thin) instead of dismissing it.
Offered to record the decision in docs/decisions.md.
